deck.title: Onboarding Team Sync
deck.tagline: Weekly review · ship updates, experiment health, and what's next.

agenda: Login A/B test — context, flows, and rollout state
Retrospective — what went well, what slowed us down

headlineFinding.kicker: Flow B is catching its target population
headlineFinding.explanation: matched a temporary-password account — invited barbers we want to catch before they hit Sign Up by mistake.
headlineFinding.takeaway: Without Flow B's email-first routing, this cohort would most likely have created accidental Indie accounts. The experiment is doing what it was designed to do at the routing level.

thisWeekShipped.title: Email-First Login A/B test live in prod
thisWeekShipped.detail: iOS 3.23.0 + Android shipped on April 30. Phased rollout: 1% → 2% → 5% → 10% → 20% → 50% over 6 days; 100% scheduled for May 7.

thisWeekShipped.title: iOS analytics naming collision fixed
thisWeekShipped.detail: PR #4138 — renamed 'login_failed' → 'Login attempt failed' to avoid clobbering the legacy event. Merged into release-3.23.0.

thisWeekShipped.title: Snowflake access unblocked + first 8 queries pulled
thisWeekShipped.detail: Two iterations same day — v1 surfaced an ID-space bug (user_id not on pre-login events), v2 used anonymous_id and gave us readable numbers.

experiment.jiraTitle: Implement onboarding login A/B test (Flow A & Flow B)
experiment.problem: Barbers who are already registered in a shop download the app and accidentally create a new shop instead of logging into their existing account. They don't realize they already have credentials set up by their shop owner.
experiment.solution: A/B test with two new login flow variants designed to guide existing barbers to log in instead of accidentally signing up.

flowA.name: Flow A — "Welcome back to SQUIRE!"
steps: User opens the app and sees the landing screen with the barbershop background
A login sheet automatically appears with "Welcome back to SQUIRE!" — it cannot be dismissed
User enters their email and password, then taps "Login"
"Forgot password" takes the user to password recovery, then returns to the login sheet
"Don't have an account? Sign up" takes the user to the signup page, then returns to the login sheet

flowB.name: Flow B — "Email-first"
flowB.tagline: Single email entry routes the user to the right path
steps: User sees a new landing: "The essential app for every barber" with "Join for free" and "Login" buttons
Both buttons open a sheet asking for the user's email: "Let's get you started"
Email not found → User redirected to the signup page
Email found, temporary password → "Welcome to SQUIRE, {name}!" — asks for the temporary password from invite email
Email found, existing user → "Welcome back, {name}!" — asks for their password
Temp-password users are asked to set a new password before continuing

rollout.interpretation: Cumulative exposure for the first 6 days was small (≤20% of traffic up to May 4). Today is the first day at 100% — meaningful statistical reads start accumulating from here forward.

experiment.methodologyNote: There is no in-experiment control group. Comparison vs the current (pre-experiment) login flow is done via before/after analysis at the reporting layer — not within the A/B itself.
experiment.northStar: Did the rate of barbers accidentally creating Indie accounts go down?
experiment.primaryMetric: ACCOUNT_CREATED where account_type = 'indie' AND came_from = 'signup_link', segmented by variant.
experiment.successThreshold: Flow B wins if indie signups drop ≥30% without reducing login completion by more than 10%.

metrics.title: Variant balance — is the 50/50 split actually 50/50?
metrics.definition: Distinct devices (anonymous_id) assigned to each variant via LOGIN_EXPERIMENT_ASSIGNED, post-deploy.
metrics.whyItMatters: If the split is uneven, every downstream comparison is biased — a 60/40 imbalance can flip a tie into either side's win.
metrics.watchFor: Expect 50/50 ± a few percent. Outside ~45/55 is a Sample Ratio Mismatch worth investigating before trusting downstream metrics.
metrics.currentRead: Confirmed imbalance: 64% / 36% at the device level (390 Flow A vs 220 Flow B). 14 percentage points off 50/50 — consistent with LD's signal. SRM is real, root cause investigation in flight (separate from this deck).

metrics.title: Login completion funnel by variant
metrics.definition: Distinct actors at each step: assigned → welcome viewed → login succeeded. Conversion rate = succeeded ÷ welcome.
metrics.whyItMatters: Tells us whether either flow is breaking login for real users. A drop in either variant is a guardrail violation, regardless of north-star outcome.
metrics.watchFor: Flow B should not lose more than 10% completion vs Flow A.
metrics.currentRead: Flow A: 185 assigned → 28 welcome viewed → 67 succeeded. Flow B: 17 assigned → 16 welcome → 18 succeeded. Numbers don't reconcile cleanly because login_succeeded uses USER_ID while pre-login events use ANONYMOUS_ID. Directionally: Flow B's near-1:1 assigned/welcome ratio (16/17) is a positive signal that users in Flow B reach the welcome screen reliably.

metrics.title: Accidental signups — Indie via signup_link
metrics.definition: ACCOUNT_CREATED where account_type = 'indie' AND came_from = 'signup_link', segmented by variant.
metrics.whyItMatters: The metric the experiment exists to move. If it drops in Flow B vs Flow A, we shipped the right thing.
metrics.watchFor: ≥30% reduction in Flow B vs Flow A is the pre-committed success bar.
metrics.currentRead: Zero Indie signups via signup_link in either variant during this 7-day window. Either organic indie volume is low this period, or the indie account_type isn't being emitted (QA validation needed). Closest proxy: Flow A — 38 barber/signup_link signups (the original problem path). Flow B — 34 barber/email_not_found signups (the redirected path).

metrics.eyebrow: Flow B insight · The money signal
metrics.title: Email check distribution — who actually enters Flow B?
metrics.definition: EMAIL_CHECK_RESULT distribution across result (returning · temp_password · new_user) and input_type (email · username). Flow B exclusive.
metrics.whyItMatters: This is the signal Flow B was designed to extract. Email check is the routing decision — high temp_password share means Flow B is catching its target population (invited barbers) before they accidentally sign up.
metrics.watchFor: Meaningful temp_password share (>20% suggests strong target capture). High new_user share would mean accidental signups are still leaking.
metrics.currentRead: 33.5% temp_password (email) — Flow B is catching invited barbers as designed. 43.7% returning (email) — healthy returning login traffic. 14.1% new_user (email) — genuine new users. Username inputs ~9% — legacy edge.

metrics.title: Friction signals — where users struggle
metrics.definition: Counts of LOGIN_ATTEMPT_FAILED (by reason), FORGOT_PASSWORD_TAPPED (by from_state), and LOGIN_ABANDONED (by last_step_reached, iOS only). Per variant.
metrics.whyItMatters: Surfaces UX problems we wouldn't see in completion rates alone.
metrics.watchFor: Flow B should not have a higher forgot-password rate among temp_password users. Abandonment at email_entry in Flow B is a red flag if it dominates.
metrics.currentRead: Flow B introduces 57 abandonment events at the NEW email_entry step (Flow A has no equivalent). Password-step abandonment: 202 events (Flow A) vs 123 (Flow B). Forgot password — Flow A: 8 users via flow_a_sheet. Flow B: 2 returning + 1 temp + 2 strays in flow_a_sheet (known PostHog/LD pinning race). Login_failed asymmetry: only Flow B rows present (8 users, 14 events) — under investigation.

perExposure.title: Per-exposure rates tell a different story
perExposure.subtitle: Raw counts hide the SRM. Normalized, Flow B's accidental-barber rate is ~60% higher per device.

flowA.label: Flow A · accidental barber via signup_link
flowA.path: User sees Sign Up button → taps it → signs up by mistake.

flowB.label: Flow B · accidental barber via email_not_found
flowB.path: User types email → API returns not-found → auto-redirect to signup.

perExposure.honestTake: Not declaring a winner. This is an early signal worth understanding before the experiment matures.
caveats: Small volume — 7 days, most of which were at <50% rollout
Different routing — Flow A is intent click; Flow B is auto-redirect after email_not_found
ID-space mixing on funnel data not yet resolved
SRM root cause still under investigation

launchDarkly.note: LD dashboard. ~61/39 — directional signal of SRM.

snowflake.note: Snowflake confirms imbalance at device level: 64% / 36%. 14 pts off 50/50.

funnel.caveat: ID-space mixing: login_succeeded uses USER_ID, others use ANONYMOUS_ID. Directional only until next pull.

northStarData.caveat: Indie north star is at zero in both variants this window. Proxy: accidental barber signups — Flow A via signup_link, Flow B via email_not_found redirect.

details: Login_failed only appears for Flow B (13 wrong_password + 1 network_error). Flow A absence suggests older app versions emit the legacy event without the variant property.
Forgot password — Flow A: 196 events from flow_a_sheet. Flow B: 42 returning + 15 temp + 2 stray flow_a_sheet (known race condition).
Abandonment — Flow A: 202 at password_entry. Flow B: 57 at email_entry (new step) + 123 at password_entry.

caveats.title: ID-space mixing in funnel
caveats.detail: login_succeeded uses USER_ID; pre-login events use ANONYMOUS_ID via COALESCE. The funnel doesn't reconcile cleanly across steps until we use a consistent ID throughout.
caveats.fix: Re-pull funnel with ANONYMOUS_ID across all steps.

caveats.title: Anomalous repeat events on login_abandoned
caveats.detail: 1-2 distinct devices firing 100+ abandonment events each. Suggests over-firing on screen redrive.
caveats.fix: Audit iOS presenter deinit logic for spurious dismissals.

caveats.title: Anonymous_id rotation suspected on email_check_result
caveats.detail: 4-7 distinct devices firing 30+ events each. Either heavy retry behavior or anonymous_id rotates across sessions.
caveats.fix: Compare distinct anonymous_id counts vs distinct user_id counts per session window.

caveats.title: signup_link_tapped scope leak
caveats.detail: 3 events appeared in Flow B (should be Flow A only). 2 distinct devices.
caveats.fix: Audit firing site — likely a scope guard missing or a flag-flip race.

caveats.title: Login_failed asymmetry — only Flow B rows
caveats.detail: Flow A returns zero rows with the variant filter. Either older app versions don't emit variant on the legacy event, or Flow A failures are landing in a different table.
caveats.fix: Cross-check LOGIN_ATTEMPT_FAILED next pull, audit which app versions emit which event.

caveats.title: Indie signups = 0 in window
caveats.detail: Both variants show zero ACCOUNT_CREATED with account_type='indie'. Either organic indie volume is low this period, or the property isn't being set.
caveats.fix: QA-validate property emission for indie account creation flow.

cells.label: Platforms · iOS + Android shipped in parallel

wentWell.title: Cross-platform parity at the wire level
wentWell.detail: iOS and Android emit identical event names + property values byte-for-byte. One Snowflake query covers both platforms.

wentWell.title: Phased rollout managed risk
wentWell.detail: 1% → 2% → 5% → 10% → 20% → 50% → 100% over 7 days. No production incidents during the ramp.

wentWell.title: Analytics plan locked before code shipped
wentWell.detail: Alignment with Tristan on north star + 9 events + threshold + caveats — guards against rationalizing post-hoc.

wentWell.title: Snowflake unblock → actionable data same day
wentWell.detail: Went from 'waiting for access' to 'first headline finding in deck' within a single working session. Two query iterations same day.

slowedDown.title: Unknowns surfaced during QA, not before
slowedDown.detail: Khem (QA) flagged that login needed to accept usernames, not just emails. iOS had an auto sign-in flow that wasn't tested → required late fix and delayed deploy.
slowedDown.learning: Pre-QA spike to enumerate edge cases (legacy user types, auto-flows already in place) before implementation locks in.

slowedDown.title: Concurrent feature-flag tooling migration
slowedDown.detail: LaunchDarkly → PostHog → GrowthBook in motion at the same time. Wiring overhead, decisions about which to use, source-of-truth confusion.
slowedDown.learning: Lock the analytics destination before instrumenting; treat tooling migration as a separate workstream.

slowedDown.title: Cross-platform context switching on unfamiliar code
slowedDown.detail: Both iOS and Android were ramp-up territory. Ship work doubles when you're not the expert on either.
slowedDown.learning: Mitigation in place: future onboarding work moves to React Native — familiar territory, better predictability for timelines.

slowedDown.title: Side project (Weekly Digest) pulled focus
slowedDown.detail: Bugs from Reporting team kept being assigned, eating dev time on this experiment.
slowedDown.learning: Solution applied: 1 day/week allocated to the Digest project; new dev from Artur's team to handle going forward.

slowedDown.title: Access provisioning not pre-staged
slowedDown.detail: Snowflake + flag service accesses surfaced as bottlenecks AFTER the experiment shipped, delaying the first analytics pull by days.
slowedDown.learning: File access requests on day 1 of project, not when needed.

commitments.text: Re-pull data with consistent ANONYMOUS_ID across all funnel steps
Investigate root cause of SRM (64/36 device split confirmed in Snowflake)
commitments.note: Snowflake = source of truth, per Yurii. LD verdict ('flowB best') based on misconfigured primary metric — irrelevant.

commitments.text: Investigate bugs surfaced this session — signup_link scope leak, login_abandoned over-firing, login_failed asymmetry
Build login flows (A and B) in the new RN app — collaborate with Appointments team
commitments.note: When we decide a winner, RN implementation is ready to ship.

commitments.text: Iterate Courses project with Tristan — prototype using Claude Design / OSS alternative on existing Figma
Roadmap doc in Confluence for Courses (analog to the Analytics Plan we wrote for login)
Continue weekly analytics extraction (this deck) with fresh data
Align on RN app dates — May 29: full feature parity? Onboarding-only first? — confirm with Eric / Yurii

courses.title: Courses · indie onboarding next chapter
courses.tagline: Tristan's plan #2: indie onboarding restructured around course-based education.
courses.context: Per Tristan's strategic plan from team kickoff, the next big project after Login is restructuring indie onboarding around courses. Already kicked off pre-experiment; on hold during Tristan's vacation. Now resuming.

figma.name: Soooooo Onboarding
figma.subtitle: Indie course-based education board
highlights: Detail screens for indie barber path

plan: Take Tristan's existing Figma as input
Feed into Claude Design / OSS alternative — auto-extracts design system, generates live HTML
Iterate prototype rapidly with stakeholders (Eric, Yurii, Tristan)
Once validated, code into the RN auth shell when it lands

tools.name: Claude Design
tools.note: Two-pane interface: chat on the left, live HTML canvas on the right. Reads Figma + codebases to extract design systems. Powered by Claude Opus 4.7.

tools.name: Open CoDesign
tools.note: Local-first, BYOK. Imports Claude Code API key. Multi-model: Claude / GPT / Gemini / Kimi / Ollama.

tools.name: OpenPencil
tools.note: Reads .fig natively. Headless CLI + MCP server for AI agents. Best fit for our Figma → code workflow.

tools.name: DESIGN.md
tools.note: Markdown spec for brand consistency across agents. Drop in repo root → every compatible agent reads it.

step1.title: Figma board
step1.detail: Tristan's existing 'Soooooo Onboarding' Figma — already structured around courses, indie path, celebration moments.

step2.title: Claude Design / OpenPencil
step2.detail: Two-pane interface: prompt + live canvas. Reads Figma to extract design system. We refine via inline comments and prompts.

step3.title: Live HTML prototype
step3.detail: Clickable, testable prototype to share with stakeholders. Once validated, we move to code in the RN auth shell.

openIssues.title: Re-run funnel with consistent ANONYMOUS_ID throughout
openIssues.status: Queries v3 drafted. Will pull tomorrow with full-traffic day-1 data.

openIssues.title: Sample Ratio Mismatch confirmed at device level
openIssues.status: Snowflake confirms 64/36 device split. Investigation in flight.

openIssues.title: Privacy sign-off on user_id property
openIssues.status: Flagged in PR descriptions. Owner not yet identified.

openIssues.title: signup_link_tapped scope leak (3 Flow B events)
openIssues.status: Sanity check failed — needs audit of firing site.

openIssues.status: Held back pending iOS validation in prod.
